compiled_state.description:
# Outsider onboarding: a warm "your first change" tutorial layered on CONTRIBUTING.md

## Why

CONTRIBUTING.md §1–§3 already documents the external Gerrit path (GitHub OAuth, clone-from-Gerrit-as-origin, hook, refs/for/main, the two votes, the §2e submit-authorization callout) — the gap is not information but SHAPE: it is a complete reference, not a first-contact narrative, and nothing on GitHub funnels an outsider into it. Every surveyed Gerrit-keeping project ships a dedicated first-change tutorial distinct from the reference (Go's contribute guide, LibreOffice GetInvolved, Wikimedia Gerrit/Tutorial); JGit proves a web-OAuth Gerrit needs only a short one. This story adds the tutorial layer and the funnel links; it does NOT restructure CONTRIBUTING.md's reference content.

## What

- New `docs/your-first-change.md`, 800–1500 words (one authoritative range; target ~1000), Go's two-layer structure (one-time setup vs per-change loop), LibreOffice-warm tone, consistent with CONTRIBUTING.md §1–§2 (same commands, same remote naming — the outsider clone uses `origin` = Gerrit, so pushes are `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/main`; never the internal dual-remote `gerrit` name):
  1. How review works here (3 sentences): Gerrit not PRs; two bot votes gate submit; GitHub is a mirror. For vote semantics, link CONTRIBUTING.md §2c now; the review-policy story (which depends on this one) will retarget that link to `docs/review-policy.md` when it lands — this tutorial does NOT duplicate the vote table (epic single-source rule).
  2. One-time setup (~5 min): sign in at rebar.solutions.navateam.com with GitHub OAuth → generate HTTP password (Settings → HTTP Credentials) → clone via the authenticated `/a/` URL → install the commit-msg hook (one sentence demystifying Change-Id). Include the two common account-setup failure states (OAuth loop / hook curl failing) as gotcha rows.
  3. Your first change: branch from main → commit with `git commit -s` (DCO sign-off; brief, links CONTRIBUTING's DCO section once the DCO story lands — until then links developercertificate.org) AND the `rebar-ticket:` trailer. The outsider ticket flow, specified in-scope (no deferral): open a GitHub issue describing the work; the maintainer files the rebar ticket and replies with the id to put in the trailer; for tiny fixes the shepherded-patch path (CONTRIBUTING) skips Gerrit entirely. → `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/main` with the magic ref demystified → reading a −1: link the authoritative vote-semantics doc; summarize only "finding = fix and re-push; coverage-gap = infra, not your code; Verified −1 = CI, comment `recheck` for flakes" without enumerating sub-reasons (the enum lives in one place) → `git commit --amend --no-edit` + re-push → final step: "a maintainer submits once both votes are green — you don't need (and won't see) a Submit button until you're added to Contributors" (mirrors CONTRIBUTING §2e; no false promise the outsider lands it themselves).
  4. Gotchas table: missing Change-Id; pushed to `main` instead of `refs/for/main`; new commit instead of amend; missing sign-off; missing/unresolvable rebar-ticket trailer; CI flake; OAuth/hook setup failures.
  5. See-also → CONTRIBUTING.md as the full reference.
- CONTRIBUTING.md: light edits only — add a prominent link to the tutorial near the top ("first time? start here"); no restructuring (the reference is already externally accurate).
- `docs/README.md` (audience-indexed docs ToC): add the tutorial under the Contributor section.
- `.github/pull_request_template.md`: add the tutorial link alongside the existing CONTRIBUTING pointer (the PR template is an outsider's first contact until the lockdown bot lands).

## Scope

IN: `docs/your-first-change.md` (new), CONTRIBUTING.md top-link, `docs/README.md` index entry, PR-template link.
OUT: README.md front-door work (own story: README front door); restructuring CONTRIBUTING.md; the DCO section and shepherded-patch section (owned by the DCO and PR-posture stories, which add their own cross-links INTO this tutorial when they land — ownership-inversion rule); vote-semantics table (review-policy story); video/asciinema tutorials; changes to review gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/your-first-change.md` exists — prove: `wc -w docs/your-first-change.md` in [800, 1500]; covers the nine-beat sequence (account → credential → clone → hook → branch → signed commit w/ trailer → push refs/for → respond → amend/re-push) — prove: grep hits for `refs/for/main`, `commit-msg`, `--amend`, `commit -s`, `rebar-ticket:`.
- [ ] Zero Nava-internal references on the happy path — prove: `grep -cE "feature-branch-drivers|CLAUDE|dual-remote|push gerrit " docs/your-first-change.md` = 0 (the push remote is `origin`).
- [ ] The outsider ticket flow is actionable without sibling stories: the GitHub-issue → maintainer-files-ticket → trailer loop is fully described in this doc.
- [ ] Submit expectations match reality — prove: doc states a maintainer performs Submit for first-time contributors (no instruction that the outsider presses Submit).
- [ ] No duplicated vote table — prove: the doc links CONTRIBUTING §2c (or review-policy.md once retargeted) and does not enumerate coverage-gap sub-reasons.
- [ ] Funnel links present — prove, one grep PER FILE (multi-file grep -q passes if any one matches, so each is its own check): `grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -q "your-first-change" docs/README.md`; `grep -q "your-first-change" .github/pull_request_template.md`.
compiled_state.title: Outsider onboarding: your-first-change tutorial, CONTRIBUTING restructure, README front door
